title: The One With All The Rugby

lines.text: A beauty parlour, Rachel is getting a manicure while Chandler, yes Chandler, is getting a petticure (Does that scare you that I know those terms?  Well, it scares me.).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I can't believe I'm getting my nails done!  And you said it was gonna be fun! (pause) Which it kinda is.  Also, you said there would be other guys here. There are no other guys here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, there's a guy right over there. (Points to the counter)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's a mailman! That's our mailman! (Waves to the mailman) (Sarcastic) Hi. How are ya?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, don't worry! This doesn't make you any less of a guy! (Chandler starts blowing on his fingernails like women do.) That does! (Chandler stops blowing.) What am I sitting on? (She looks and finds a huge nail.) I hate to think what this woman was scratching when this broke off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know who used to have nails like that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: OH...MY...GAWD!! (Yep, you guessed it. It's Janice.)
Opening Credits
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Joey are standing at the counter. Monica is flipping a light switch on and off next to the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey let me ask you a question. What does this light switch do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, Nothing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Didn't it drive you crazy to have a switch and not know what it did?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know what it did! Nothing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They wouldn't have put it there if it didn't do something! How can you not care?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like this. (Shrugs)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, here's another question for ya. Uhh, do you know what that silver knob on the toilet does?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure! It flushes it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, good. Now that since you know, when you come over would you mind actually using it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, with Janice in tow) Hello!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (Sees Janice.) Ah!! (Janice screams in surprise.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Guess who we ran into today?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Janice?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Isn't this amazing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How have you been?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh well, I'm divorced.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh, wow.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Yeah, I'm riding the alimony pony. (Does the now famous laugh.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And there it is.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I just came up to say, "Hi!" Hi! (to Chandler) And you, sweetie, I'll see you tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Bye.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: B-bye!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye. (Finally closes the door on her.) (After it's closed) I can't stand the woman! (Phoebe is shocked, Joey is relieved.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! I thought you were crazy about her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know, but all of those little annoying things she did before we fell in love? Like her voice, her laugh, her personality-Well, they're all back! Y'know? And she's picked up like nine new ones!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what are you doing bringing her here?! There's people here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't worry about it. I'm taking care of it tonight. (Chandler opens the fridge and grabs something to drink.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are not. You have never been able to break up with her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (standing in the door of the fridge) Well, I don't have to break up with her this time. We're not involved! I'm going to do a pre-emptive strike! I'm going to end it with her before it starts. My ass is like frozen! (Closes the fridge.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, try sticking it in the freezer for 20 minutes. (They all look around and then back at Joey.) I'm tellin' ya!
A street, Ross and Emily are walking home from a date.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I can't believe you really walk alone here! I mean, you hear such stories about New York.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it's really not that bad. I mean, I-I for one, feel perfectly safe.
At that moment two very large men start screaming and running towards Emily and picking her up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Help! Help!! Help! Help!!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: No, no, no Ross! Ross, these are friends of mine from home. (Introducing them) Liam, Devon, this is Ross.

lines.speaker: Devon
lines.text: Hey, mate.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: How are ya man?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, that was a good one, huh? (Imitating himself) Help! Help!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: So how are you? I've been meaning to ring you ever since I arrived but umm, well, I've been rather busy.

lines.speaker: Devon
lines.text: Do you realise that we have not seen each other since the night of that U2 concert?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh my God. I think you're right.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: (puts his arm around her) Well, actually the last time you and I saw each other was that morning.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, Liam. (Ross laughs and takes her back.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Liam. So uh, what, were you guys playing soccer or something-or should I call it (In an English accent) football?

lines.speaker: Devon
lines.text: We were playing rugby.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: In fact we're playing a game at the park tomorrow. You're welcome to play too if you want.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (laughs) Ross play rugby? I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's ah, what's so funny about that?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Well I mean, you're American to start with. You don't even have rugby here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we didn't have freedom here until 1776, either so...

lines.speaker: Devon
lines.text: So good then! We'll see you at Riverside Park at 2:00! Cheers!

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Cheers!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cheers!
Liam and Devon both take a swig of their beers, while Ross takes a swig of his coffee. The Brits both crush their cans, and not to be out done, Ross crushes his coffee cup, spilling its contents.
Central Perk, Chandler is attempting his pre-emptive strike.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh boy, I just love to sing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I-I know that you do, but I think one of the reasons people were complaining though, was that they paid to hear the actor sing Old Man River.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, look at us! Who would've thought that Cupid had a station at 14th Street Nails. (Does the laugh)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs) Okay, we have to talk. I'm just getting out of a very serious relationship...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I know! And I'm just getting out of a marriage, I mean talk about meant to be!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right! I just think that this is happening too soon.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, too soon, too schmoon. Face it honey, I am not letting you get away this time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I hear ya. (Pause) But! Unfortunately, my company is transferring me overseas!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh no! Where to? (Gasps) Too Paris?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! Not, Paris.
Janice:Too London? No-no, Rome? Vienna? Ooh-ooh, Barcelona?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, could you just stop talking for a second? (Thinks) Yemen. That's right, yes, I'm being transferred to Yemen!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: When?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know exactly.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ugh, well I will just have to soak up every once of Chandler Bing until that moment comes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But I do know that it's some time tomorrow.
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe and Monica are plugging in a bunch of electronics.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Done?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering, with Joey) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! What's up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This switch thing has been driving me crazy. So I turned it off and checked every outlet. Now, four of them don't work. Which means, one of them has to be controlled by the switch. So, I plugged in things in all four of the outlets that-that make noise, so that way, when I turn it on I just follow the noise and find out which one it is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Rachel) I bet I stopped listening before you did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, you-you also could've used uh, lamps and then followed the light.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well, I'm using noise. Okay. All right! So, is everybody ready? Here we go. (She flips on the switch and a hum starts.) I hear something! I hear something! Where is it? (They all start looking until Rachel realises it's Joey.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's coming from Joey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, that's so freaky! Turn him off!!
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Ross, Joey, and Rachel are watching rugby on TV as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, hey, could we put on the news? I think it might be raining.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, just hold on a second. I'm watching this rugby thing on ESPN. I don't know what the big deal is. I'm man enough to play this sport.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, you're not even man enough to order the channel that carries the sport.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (entering) Hey there Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (shocked) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (whispering to Ross) Janice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know uh, you didn't really have to help me pack.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ohh, well when you said all you were going to be doing between now and the time you leave is packing, you didn't really leave me much choice. Did you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I-I thought I did but, I-I guess I did not!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I'm packing. Y'know I'm-I'm packing 'cause I'm moving to Yemen tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks for telling me!
Janice runs into the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm only going to pretend I'm moving to Yemen, it's the only way I can get rid off her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhhh, good one! And Yemen that actually sounds like a real country.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (leaning in from the bedroom) Chandler! Come on, I'm gonna show how to roll up your underwear and stuff it in your shoes. It's a real space saver.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I do that 'cause it makes me look taller.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Okay, Chandler, come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Janice) Okay. (to Joey) Joey, trade lives with me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope. (To Ross) Man look at this! Ross, I can't believe you said you'd play rugby. I mean look how brutal this is!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I can handle it! All right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please, Ross, you-you got hurt playing badminton with my dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's 'cause-'cause you're mom's dog kept-kept looking at me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pointing to the TV) Okay, Ross, look-look-look-look, look right here. That's called a scrum, okay? It's kinda like a huddle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And is a hum, kinda like a scruddle?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross! (Laughs) They're gonna kill you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, why are you doing this anyway?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you should've seen the guy that she used to go out with. I mean, he's like Joe Rugby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're kidding! And he plays rugby?! That's so funny. (Realises) Ohh! I see how you did that. All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway, she thought the very idea of me playing rugby with him was like hilarious. So I'm gonna show her how tough I really am!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starts laughing, Ross stares at her) I'm sorry. I'm sorry. You're right, you are a tough guy. You're the toughest palaeontologist I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, come on look, Ross can take care of himself! It's not like he's...Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (from his bedroom) Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, don't worry about me. Okay? I'll just stay real energetic and stay away from the ball. I'll uh, I'll be that guy right out of the circle. (He points to a player who starts running and then gets viscously tackled from behind.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well maybe there was a dog lookin' at him.
Ross turns and wants to attack Rachel, but Joey stops him.
Commercial Break
The park, everyone is warming up for the rugby game. Only Joey and Phoebe came to support Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross-Ross-Ross-Ross! Stay away from that guy (Points), and that guy (Points). And that one-Dude! They're all huge!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They don't look any bigger than me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, maybe that's because you're closer to you. So you look bigger to you from where you are.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I'm just going to say hi to the lads. All right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Okay. (Goes to say hi to the lads.)
A player comes over and picks up a ball in front of Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the player) Hi.
The player stands up and smiles. Showing that he has no front teeth.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoa! (The player leaves and to Joey) I kinda liked it.
The referee blows the whistle and the players gather to start the game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I know what I have to do. I've got to go Red Ross. (Joey and Phoebe don't know what he's talking about.) Y'know, Red Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I totally don't know what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! The time we were all waiting in line for Dances With Wolves and that one guy cut in line in front of us and I just lost it?! Screamed at him! Turned all red! Red Ross!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You'll see.
[Cut to Emily, Devon, and Liam]

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Liam, do me a favour. Tell the lads to go easy on Ross, it's his first time.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: You don't say! (We see Ross who is hopping about with the ball and spikes it in his face.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (to Ross) Good luck, babe.
The scrum forms and the game is underway.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Ross! Ross! Come on! Get in here! (Ross gets pumped up.) Ross! Come on!
Ross walks over to the scrum, walks around a bit looking for a way to get into the scrum.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Ross, come on! Get in the bloody scrum! Ross, get in!
Ross, urged on by his team-mates, jumps on top of the scrum and falls headfirst into the middle, leaving his feet sticking straight up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: JOEY!!!!!!
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Monica is going over some plans as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You...are...not going to believe it! Joshua came into work today, and guess what happened?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He asked you out?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. But I was showing him some cufflinks and I felt his pulse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Saucy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (refers to the table) What are these?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Electrical plans for the building.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay should I be scared?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know that switch does something, okay? So-so I went down to city hall and got these. All I had to do was pay $25 and wait in line for three hours.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! If only more people knew.
The airport, the Yemen Express counter, Chandler is still trying to get rid of Janice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know you, really didn't have to take me to the airport.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh please. Every moment is precious. Y'know? Besides, somebody had to ride in that other taxi with the rest of your luggage, and your friends don't really seem to care too much that you're leaving.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we're really not that close. (Pause) Okay, so I guess this is uh, good-bye then.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: On no! No! It's not good-bye, I'm not leaving until you get on that plane.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Then I guess it's just, wait here then. (Hands her the sign that says, Wait Here.") (To the ticket agent) Hi. I need one fake ticket to Yemen.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: One ticket to Yemen?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, no. No, no, no, I just, I just need a pretend ticket.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: I'm sorry sir, I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What would you give to a kid if he wanted a ticket to play with?

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: Are you travelling with a child?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. All right, y'know what, she's (Points to Janice) gonna think that I'm handing you a credit card, but what I'm really gonna do is hand you a library card.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: Ah, sir a ticket to Yemen is $2,100 and we don't take library cards.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: What's the matter? Is something wrong? Do you have to stay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to the ticket agent) American Express?
The rugby game, Ross is getting killed.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I can't believe they're doing that to him! I told them to go easy on him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No offence but, y'know sometimes it's hard to understand you, y'know with the accent, so...
The whistle blows.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: That's just halftime, there's more of this.
Ross limps over all covered in mud.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you see me? I was pretty good, huh? That is one fun game!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Emily) Hey, could you do me a favour? Could you just grab me a bottle of water?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. (When she's gone he collapses into Joey.) I-I think I'm dying. I really do.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, poor baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Tell my son that I love him. (Emily returns with the water.) Excellent! Well, okay, I gotta have some more fun!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ross, they are killing you out there!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (whines "No.") That's not true!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She's right! You have to stop!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? No! No, I'm not stopping. I'm Red Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, if you go back out there, you're gonna be Dead Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't care! I am not quitting! I insist on finishing this game!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: All right, all right, if you insist on doing this, at least let me help you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, God no! That is no place for a woman. Those guys will grab anything.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: No. That's not what I'm saying. I just may know a few things that might help you inflict some pain.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I like that.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yeah? Listen closely, Devon has got a weak ankle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: One swift kick and he'll back off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, bad ankle, got it!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: And that big bloke with the beard, he has got a trick hip. Yeah. And uh, and David over there, I heard he doesn't wear a cup.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? I can use that, trick hip, no cup, okay! Okay!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: And uh, Liam, Liam's got bad knees. You hit him right and he'll go down like a lamp.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But-but, Liam's on my team.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I don't care! You just get him!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna go get him! Okay, I am going back in! (Squeals like a madman.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The Red Ross! Okay.
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Monica is reading a book as Rachel returns.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (noticing a bunch of pictures around the door that weren't there originally.) What-what are-what are these?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, just some pictures I made and hung up. I thought they'd brighten up the place. They do don't you think?
Rachel rips one of the wall and finds a huge hole underneath.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no-no, no!
Rachel rips off another one, revealing another hole. Rachel then moves onto a third one, but this one doesn't have a hole underneath it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know that there's no hole there, I just really liked that picture.
Rachel looks at it and then throws it away. She then removes a fourth one, revealing a third hole.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Look at this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, but there is a wire back there! I mean that switch is connected to something!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care! The wires have come loose in your head!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just thought that if I could follow the wire I could find out what it did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And did you?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. It disappears back there behind that baseboard. For a minute there, I thought it went downstairs.
Rachel removes a paper on the floor which is covering a hole and gasps.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But it didn't. Say hello to Mrs. Chatracus.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Chatracus
lines.text: Hello darling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello, Mrs. Chatracus.
Central Perk, They are returning from the rugby game, Joey and Emily are carrying Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now, are you sure you don't want to go see a doctor?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no! That-that'll just bring me down! This was great! I mean I-I-I was great! This is a great day! Y'know what? I'm buying everyone coffee. All right? If someone would just grab my wallet, it's in my pocket.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, not you. (Emily gets it.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, look, your eye's still popping out a little, I'm gonna go get some ice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, ice! I am so in the mood for ice! (They go and get the coffee and the ice leaving Ross and Emily alone.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You were amazing out there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I kinda was, wasn't I?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I made a man twice my size cry. I mean, I haven't done that since I was four and I washed my dad's Porsche with rocks.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You really enjoyed yourself didn't you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Please! Are you kidding? I-I hurt three huge men, I gave a guy a bloody nose-I mean I-I'm not proud of it but, I really am. And it's all because of you, wonderful, amazing you.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I think you've got concussion.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, I'm serious. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You're welcome. (She hugs him tightly and he winces.) I'm sorry. Did I hurt you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's worth the pain. (She goes to hug him again.) Y'know what, you know what? It's not.
The airport, the flight to Yemen is being called.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: (on the P.A.) This is the final boarding call for Flight 664 to Yemen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I-I guess I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, my Bing-a-ling. I'll wait for you. Do you even know how long you're going to be gone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, just until we find an energy source to replace fuel.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh. Well, I'll right you everyday. (Reading the address) 15 Yemen Road, Yemen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, good-bye. Good-bye.
He gives the agent his ticket and walks onto the jetway. Janice walks over and looks out the window. Chandler walks back into the terminal and tries to walk right past Janice, but she sees him.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice! There you are! There you are! I had to have one last kiss, and also-also you said that you were going to leave right after I got on the plane!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: No! No! I wanna see you take-off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I then guess I'm going to Yemen! I'm going to Yemen! (To this old woman also going to Yemen.) When we get to Yemen, can I stay with you?
Closing Credits
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Monica has finally given up on her search to find what the light switch does and is now flicking it on and off aimlessly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. The super couldn't figure out what it did. A $200 an hour electrician couldn't figure out what it did. I've had seven pretty serious shocks. I officially give up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess Joey was right, it does nothing.

[Cut to Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Phoebe are watching TV. The TV is turning off and on, and each time Phoebe is blinking her eyes like the Genie did. The switch obviously controls the outlet which the TV is plugged into.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See? I'm doing it. I am totally doing it. (Suddenly it stops working.) I lost it.
End
